--- title: "[CensoringType]{.doc-object-name .doc-class .doc-label .doc-label-enum}" body-classes: doc-api-page page-navigation: false html-table-processing: none --- ::: {.doc-subject} The censoring mechanism of a `Surv` response. ::: ::: {.doc-usage-source} Usage [Source](https://github.com/rich-iannone/greenwood/blob/main/greenwood/_surv.py#L43-L75){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} ::: ::: {.doc-signature .doc-Kind.CLASS} ```python CensoringType() ``` ::: ::: {.doc-text} Each member corresponds to one of the `Surv` constructors and describes how incomplete observations are handled: - `RIGHT`: Classic right-censoring. All subjects enter at time 0, and some are lost before the event occurs (`Surv.right()`). - `LEFT`: Left-censoring. The event is known to have occurred before the observation time (`Surv.left()`). - `INTERVAL`: Interval-censoring. The event is known to lie within a time bracket (`Surv.interval()`). - `COUNTING`: Counting-process (start, stop] intervals with possible late entry and time-varying covariates (`Surv.counting()`). A constructed response reports its censoring type through `Surv(...).type`, which is one of these values. ::: ## Examples {.doc-examples} List all available censoring types: ::: {#32eeddee .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} from greenwood import CensoringType list(CensoringType) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ``` [, , , ] ``` ::: ::: ## Attributes {.doc-attributes}
Name Description
COUNTING The censoring mechanism of a `Surv` response.
INTERVAL The censoring mechanism of a `Surv` response.
LEFT The censoring mechanism of a `Surv` response.
RIGHT The censoring mechanism of a `Surv` response.
